things.name: Wolverhampton Art Gallery
things.description: Award-winning free gallery with one of the UK's finest Pop Art collections featuring Peter Blake and Andy Warhol, alongside Victorian paintings and contemporary works

things.name: Bantock House Museum
things.description: Edwardian manor house museum in a park setting with superb collections of Japanware, painted enamels and domestic life displays from the Arts and Crafts era

things.name: Wightwick Manor (National Trust)
things.description: Outstanding Pre-Raphaelite Victorian manor with original William Morris wallpapers, stained glass and a collection of Dante Gabriel Rossetti paintings

things.name: Wolverhampton City Centre
things.description: Historic market town centre with the Victorian Market Hall, St. Peter's Church and the famous Wolverhampton Wanderers football statue on Queen Square

things.name: Black Country Living Museum
things.description: Extraordinary open-air industrial heritage museum reconstructing the 19th-century Black Country with working foundries, chain shops and a canal arm

things.name: Dudley Castle and Zoo
things.description: Medieval castle ruins on a hilltop above a popular zoo with a remarkable chalk cave system and views across the Black Country

things.name: West Park
things.description: Victorian park with a boating lake, bandstand and conservatory; one of the finest public parks in the Midlands and a venue for summer concerts

things.name: Bilston Craft Gallery
things.description: Free craft and design gallery in the historic enamelling town of Bilston with changing exhibitions of ceramics, jewellery and contemporary applied arts

things.name: St. Peter's Collegiate Church
things.description: 14th-century collegiate church in the city centre with a tall tower, unusual canopied pillars and the memorial to King George V's nurse Edith Cavell

things.name: Molineux Stadium
things.description: Historic home of Wolverhampton Wanderers since 1889 with 32,050 capacity; Wolves reached the Europa League quarter-finals in 2019-20 under Nuno Espirito Santo
